todaysEvents.summary: Iran formally rejected the US 15-point peace plan on Mar 26, calling it 'maximalist and unreasonable'; issued 5 counter-conditions including a complete halt to all US/Israeli aggression, war reparations, and explicit Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz (Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, NPR). (Mar 26)
todaysEvents.delta: Iran rejects US peace plan; demands Hormuz sovereignty

todaysEvents.summary: Israel's UN Ambassador Danny Danon stated Israel is 'not part of US-Iran talks' and that military operations will continue until Iran's nuclear and missile capabilities are fully eliminated; Hezbollah cluster missile struck a residential neighbourhood in Kiryat Shmona (Al Jazeera Day 26, Mar 26). (Mar 26)
todaysEvents.delta: Israel ops continue; Lebanon front re-activated

todaysEvents.summary: Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif offered to host US-Iran negotiations in Islamabad on Mar 26 — the only active diplomatic track remaining after Iran rejected the US plan; Iran's 5 counter-conditions leave door open for indirect talks but formally ruled out direct US-Iran negotiations (Reuters, Al Jazeera). (Mar 26)

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Iran's 5 counter-conditions (Mar 26) — including Hormuz sovereignty and war reparations — form the basis of indirect negotiations via Pakistan. A partial framework emerges: Iran maintains non-hostile Hormuz passage, US refrains from power plant strikes beyond Mar 28, and both sides enter Pakistan-hosted talks. Brent retreats to $90-100/bbl within 3-4 weeks on confirmed diplomatic process; JKM pullback to $18-20 as Qatar damage is assessed. P&I insurance restoration begins only on confirmed ceasefire — not on diplomatic framework alone. Key risk: Israel has explicitly stated it is not bound by US-Iran talks.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Iran's rejection of the 15-point plan effectively invalidates the Mar 28 deadline framework. Trump refrains from resuming power plant strikes but Iran's counter-conditions (Hormuz sovereignty, reparations) are unacceptable to Washington. Pakistan-hosted talks begin but make no substantive progress within 2 weeks. Hormuz non-hostile framework remains narrow — commercial P&I cover not restored. Israel continues strikes on Tehran and Iran; Hezbollah sustains Lebanon front. Brent settles $100-120/bbl; JKM at $20-24/MMBtu with no Qatar Ras Laffan restart. iTraxx Asia IG holds 170-185bp.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Trump interprets Iran's rejection as a negotiating failure and resumes power plant strikes after Mar 28. Iran revokes non-hostile Hormuz framework; full commercial blockade reinstated. IRGC resumes full offensive operations against US/Saudi targets. Lebanon front expands. Ras Laffan offline for the quarter; Strait of Malacca closure threat reactivated. Oil hits $160-190/bbl; LNG spot markets seize. ASEAN recession risk surges — Thailand and Philippines face double-digit fuel inflation and sovereign credit-rating action.
